Title: Cross-provider survey of which subscription tiers enforce client allowlists

KEY PATTERN DISCOVERED — clean economic rule that predicts every observed case: "If you pay a flat-rate subscription you get a client allowlist. If you pay per-token you do not." Anchor-priced subscription tiers (0-100/mo for 00+/mo equivalent API usage) must gate misuse via UA/OAuth allowlists; pay-per-token APIs price misuse out directly so they do not need gating. STRICT ALLOWLIST (do not spoof, termination risk): Moonshot Kimi Code Token Plan (CONFIRMED TERMINATED our account), Anthropic Claude Pro/Max OAuth (banned third-party tools Feb 2026, reinstated with credit-pool catch June 2026), Zhipu GLM Coding Plan sk-sp- keys (TOS prohibits automated scripts/custom backends), Alibaba Qwen Coding Plan (TOS: intended only for Claude Code / Qwen Code; not curl/Postman/Dify). ADVISORY/LAX (TOS has allowlist but enforcement is currently permissive): Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan (xaxiu-harness/1.0 works TODAY but could tighten any time — matches Kimi pre-termination posture). OPEN (no client allowlist, safe by design — all pay-per-token APIs): DeepSeek API, Kimi Platform PAYG (api.moonshot.ai), Anthropic API key path (not OAuth), OpenAI API, Gemini API, MiMo PAYG (sk- keys), Doubao, Zhipu GLM PAYG (standard endpoint). AGGREGATORS (most lax — designed for arbitrary clients): OpenRouter (org-level model/provider allowlists only, no UA gate), Together AI, Fireworks, Groq, Cerebras Inference, Hyperbolic, Hugging Face Inference, Replicate. APPLIED TO HARNESS POOL: (1) DeepSeek = keep as primary (PAYG, open). (2) Kimi tp- = drop per W14-KIMI-REPLACEMENT-WITH-GLM. (3) MiMo tp- = keep with caveat; consider sk- PAYG insurance key. Title: Subprocess engine adapter: dispatch via local claude binary for TOS-compliant subscription-tier access
Notes: Pattern B implementation per operator directive 2026-05-25 evening: "I want to proceed with Pattern B, I don't mind getting a new subscription of kimi to try this again, but believe you can try setting it up with mimo first." WHAT IT IS: new engine adapter (src/harness/engines/claude_code_subprocess.py) that dispatches via the LOCAL claude CLI binary as a subprocess instead of direct httpx calls. The actual HTTP request comes from Claude Code carrying its legitimate claude/<version> User-Agent — which IS on Moonshot/Xiaomi/Zhipu/DeepSeek allowlists. Zero spoofing, TOS-compliant access to subscription-tier providers. WIRE PATH: subprocess.run([claude, --print, --bare, --model M, --output-format json, --no-session-persistence, --permission-mode auto, --max-budget-usd N]) with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL + ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env vars redirecting at the provider. --bare strips session state / hooks / plugins / keychain / CLAUDE.md so dispatch is deterministic. LIVE SMOKE TEST (2026-05-25 evening) against MiMo Token Plan via subprocess: success=True in 8.1s; text=OK; provider-reported cost /usr/bin/bash.0098; tokens_in 1847 / tokens_out 22; UA on the wire was claude/2.1.150 (legitimate, on Xiaomi allowlist). /usr/bin/bash.014 burned across two smoke tests total. ENGINE LANDSCAPE: PROVIDER_ANTHROPIC_ENDPOINTS maps 5 routes: mimo-tp-sgp/ams/cn (Token Plan regional), mimo-payg, kimi-via-cc (api.moonshot.cn/anthropic, ready for operator to re-acquire Kimi key), anthropic-default (default Claude Code backend). DEFAULT_MODEL_PER_ENGINE provides per-route model name. FACTORY: get_engine("mimo-via-claude") returns MimoViaClaudeCodeEngine which auto-resolves Token Plan vs PAYG endpoint from MIMO_API_KEY prefix (tp- → Token Plan, sk- → PAYG) and honors MIMO_REGION env var for tp- routing. TESTS (tests/test_claude_code_subprocess_engine.py): 36 across 9 classes covering helper functions + region/key-prefix routing + MimoViaClaudeCodeEngine constructor wiring + _build_command flags (--bare, --print, --output-format json, max-budget, model resolution) + _build_env (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/AUTH_TOKEN/API_KEY set, BEDROCK/VERTEX stripped, CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE) + dispatch parsing (success JSON / is_error JSON / timeout / FileNotFoundError / non-JSON fallback / empty stdout + nonzero exit / missing key short-circuit) + factory integration (get_engine + missing-key raises) + module constants. Mocked subprocess so tests are fast + free. COST vs DIRECT-HTTPX trade-offs: +2-5s spawn overhead per dispatch; provider-reported cost replaces estimate (HUGE win for accuracy); subscription quota burns vs PAYG meter when applicable; output is structured JSON (text + usage + cost + session_id); concurrent dispatch is process-pool-bound. Best fit: high-stakes panel work, occasional Opus-grade calls, leveraging subscription quotas. Worst fit: high-throughput bulk dispatch (use direct-httpx PAYG engines). KIMI READY: kimi-via-cc endpoint mapping in place. Operator can re-acquire a Kimi Token Plan key after the prior termination, set KIMI_API_KEY, and use kimi-via-claude through this adapter — Claude Code's legitimate UA accepted by Moonshot allowlist, no termination risk. Need a small factory addition + KimiViaClaudeCodeEngine subclass (~30 LOC, follows MimoViaClaudeCodeEngine pattern). NEXT STEPS: (a) operator runs panel script with mimo-via-claude in the engine list to verify panel integration; (b) optional: add kimi-via-claude when operator re-subscribes; (c) wire engine to harness budget meter tracking so subprocess-path spend is captured (currently it bypasses record_dispatch — fix in follow-up). Title: parallel_dispatch_with_serial_retry — parallel + serial-retry helper for panel fan-out
Notes: Production-evidenced bug: panels firing N engines in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor regularly hit RemoteProtocolError on MiMo (release-gate panel, master-plan panel, todays Pattern B evaluation panel where mimo/operational-risk failed 3/3 parallel attempts then succeeded on serial retry). Per-engine HTTP-layer retry from W13-ENGINE-RETRY-RESILIENT exists inside dispatch but burns its single attempt INSIDE the parallel race — the race itself IS the contention source. NEW MODULE src/harness/engines/parallel_dispatch.py: parallel_dispatch_with_serial_retry(tasks, max_workers, max_serial_retries, retry_cooldown_sec, on_retry) — Round 1 parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor; Rounds 2..N serial retries for transient failures only. Uses W13-ENGINE-FAILURE-VISIBILITY categorize_engine_failure to decide retry-worthiness: TRANSIENT categories (transient/endpoint-down/quota-exceeded) get retried; terminated/auth-failed/no-key/unknown-failure do NOT. Title: Fresh-machine deployment evaluation + improvement roadmap
Notes: Operator question 2026-05-26: how deployable-friendly is the harness when git-cloned; how fast can it be deployed to a new computer? Methodology: walk the actual recipe end-to-end, count steps, time each, identify friction. HEADLINE: time-to-first-successful-dispatch on fresh machine is 25-40 min for experienced dev (60-90 min for new operator dominated by provider signups). The harness HAS a pip-install story (W13-INSTALL-VERIFY gates it every PR) but five friction points remain. FRICTION 1: no unified prerequisite check - operator discovers Python 3.13+/Node 18+/git/claude-CLI ad-hoc. FRICTION 2: PATH update for ~/.harness/bin wrapper dir is manual + requires shell restart. FRICTION 3: three OS-specific prereq paths (Linux/Mac/Win) with only the Windows installer (bin/install-harness.ps1) shipped. FRICTION 4: first-launch Claude Code onboarding can stall first subprocess (mitigated by W14-PATTERN-B-SECONDARY's _ensure_onboarding_bypass for Pattern B but not for direct claude usage). FRICTION 5: 5+ separate API key env vars with no unified config or harness-keys-set wizard. PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS RANKED BY LEVERAGE/EFFORT: W14-HARNESS-DOCTOR (~3-4h) - single command summarizes prereqs/keys/wrappers/PATH/engine-health/audit/budget. Eliminates whats-missing guesswork. Highest-leverage single fix; spec written into EVALUATION.md including the exact output structure. W14-WRAPPER-AUTO-PATH (~1-2h) - add --add-to-path consent-gated flag to install-wrappers. W14-LINUX-MAC-INSTALLER (~3-4h) - sibling to existing bin/install-harness.ps1 for POSIX shells. W14-HARNESS-SETUP-WIZARD (~6-8h) - interactive guided setup combining doctor + key entry + PATH add + smoke test. W14-UNIFIED-KEY-CONFIG (~2h) - harness keys set kimi VALUE + ~/.harness/keys.json file-mode 600. W14-DEPLOY-DOCKER-IMAGE (~4-6h) - self-contained image with Python+Node+claude+harness preinstalled. VERDICT: the harness is deployable but not deploy-friendly. Built for 'one operator, one workstation, set up once' rather than 'multiple machines, deployed on demand.' If the operator wants to seriously prioritize multi-machine deployment, the right next step is W14-HARNESS-DOCTOR (3-4h) because it pays for itself starting from the second machine + is useful for debugging existing installs. Output: coord/reviews/harness-deployability/EVALUATION.md with full step-by-step recipe, time estimates per step, friction analysis, doctor/setup spec, and 6 follow-up rows.

Title: Fix doubled-brace bug in keys_ui HTML template (broke CSS+JS in browser)
Notes: Operator-reported browser screenshot after W14-HARNESS-KEYS-WEB-UI showed the keys form rendering with NO dark-theme styling and NO key rows. ROOT CAUSE: src/harness/keys_ui.py HTML_PAGE template was authored with {{ and }} (doubled braces) throughout CSS rule bodies and JS function bodies, intended as escape sequences for str.format(). But the handler uses str.replace() to inject the token — not format() — so the doubled braces remained in the served HTML. CSS rules like `* {{ box-sizing: border-box; }}` parsed as `*` selector with orphan `{` declaration and all rules dropped. JS template literals like `${{TOKEN}}` evaluated as `${object}` so /api/status fetches returned 403, leaving the row container empty. FIX: rewrote HTML_PAGE with single braces throughout CSS, JS, and the footer's PowerShell example. Added module comment explaining the replace-vs-format choice so this doesn't regress. REGRESSION GUARDS (4 new tests in TestHtmlPage): test_html_has_no_doubled_braces (rejects {{ or }} anywhere), test_html_css_block_is_valid_shaped (asserts canonical single-brace rules), test_html_js_template_literal_token_is_single_braced (asserts ${TOKEN} not ${{TOKEN}}), test_rendered_html_has_token_substituted (end-to-end replace + check no doubles remain). LIVE SMOKE: started HTTPServer in background thread, curled / with token, confirmed served body is 7917 bytes with single braces, token injected, CSS reset rule intact, row container present, JS entry call present. Suite: 2650 passed, 8 skipped, 0 failed.

Title: Security hardening of keys UI (env-var allowlist, value validation, headers, Origin check)
Notes: Operator directive 2026-05-26: 'conduct security analysis and patch of this way of inputting the api to the system'. FINDINGS: P0-1 arbitrary env-var write via POST /api/test+save (high) - handlers wrote any env_var from JSON body into os.environ + .env, enabling PATH/LD_PRELOAD/PYTHONPATH attack via stolen token + malicious local page. FIXED via allowlist check. P0-2 XSS via masked-key contents (low/self-XSS) - renderRows() interpolated item fields into innerHTML template strings. FIXED via createElement + textContent. P0-3 newline/backtick/single-quote injection in pasted keys (medium) - _write_env_file wrote raw KEY=value; source .env would evaluate dollar-paren and backticks. FIXED via reject newline/NUL/quote in values + wrap values single-quoted. P0-4 env_path resolved to cwd not repo root. FIXED via _resolve_env_path() walking up to pyproject.toml. UI shows resolved path so operator sees where Save lands. P1-1 token leak in log_message - request URL was passed to logger. FIXED via regex redaction. P1-2 missing security headers - none on any response. FIXED via _SECURITY_HEADERS dict (X-Frame-Options DENY, locked-down CSP, Referrer-Policy no-referrer, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Cache-Control no-store). P1-3 no Origin check on POSTs. FIXED via _check_origin_for_post; empty Origin allowed for non-browser clients. P1-4 .env.tmp lingers on crash. FIXED via touch+chmod 0600 before content lands + _cleanup_stale_tmp at server startup. OTHER: 64KB body cap + body-drain-before-reject to avoid Windows WinError 10053 on short-circuited POSTs; constant-time token compare via secrets.compare_digest. TESTS: 27 new across TestValidateEnvVar (4), TestValidateValue (5), TestResolveEnvPath (1), TestBuildStatus updated for new payload shape (5), TestSecurityRegression (13 - allowlist on save+test, newline/quote/NUL injection, Origin pass/fail/empty, headers on HTML+JSON, KNOWN_ENV_VARS shape, large POST). VISUAL VERIFY: playwright navigated patched UI, confirmed env-path info displays the repo .env path, all 4 security headers present, PATH-injection POST returned 400 with allowlist error. SUITE: 2676 passed, 8 skipped (1 unrelated Windows file-lock flake in test_concurrent_update_engine_health that passes in isolation).

Title: Force UTF-8 encoding in Pattern B subprocess (cp1252 codec bug on Windows)
Notes: Discovered while building the P3 quality panel: subprocess.run(text=True) on Windows defaults to cp1252 codec for encoding the input= bytes. Real bug - any operator dispatching with international/Unicode content would hit this on Windows. P3 panel first run: 3/3 dispatches FAILED at 9.8s with UnicodeEncodeError on → (right-arrow).  After fix: 3/3 OK in 50s. SHIPPED: src/harness/engines/claude_code_subprocess.py subprocess.run() call now passes encoding="utf-8", errors="replace".  UTF-8 round-trips cleanly across platforms; errors="replace" prevents crashes on any un-decodable response bytes from the provider too. TESTS: 2 new in TestUnicodeSafety: test_subprocess_called_with_utf8_encoding (pins the kwargs), test_dispatch_with_unicode_prompt (round-trips a prompt containing arrow + multiplication + currency symbols).

METHOD: scripts/swarm_stress_test.py - 4 backends (claude-mimo, claude-kimi, claude-deepseek - the new TOS-safe agentic family - plus deepseek legacy comparison) x 4 concurrency levels (4, 8, 16, 32) = 16 cells, 240 total dispatches.  Plus phase 2: mixed-backend swarm panel (12 packets across 3 backends in parallel).  Realistic short prompts (code-gen, reasoning, JSON, bullet) rotated across dispatches. HEADLINE RESULT: 252/252 OK (100%), zero rate-limit/throttle observations in stderr, linear scaling all the way to 32-way concurrency. PER-BACKEND HEADLINE NUMBERS at N=32: claude-mimo: 32/32 OK, p50 56.9s, p95 60.4s, 27.4x speedup. claude-kimi: 32/32 OK, p50 54.5s, p95 66.1s, 26.6x speedup. claude-deepseek: 32/32 OK, p50 55.1s, p95 58.3s, 29.6x speedup. deepseek (legacy): 32/32 OK, p50 37.1s, p95 44.2s, 25.9x speedup. LATENCY INSIGHT: legacy direct-REST deepseek is FASTEST at high concurrency (p50 37.1s vs claude-* at 54-57s) because the Pattern B / wrapper-subprocess startup overhead doesn't amortize as well under heavy parallelism.  Tradeoff: claude-* gives full agentic capability (tools, multi-turn) and TOS-safety; legacy gives raw speed. MIXED PANEL: 12 packets, 3 backends, 39s wall, 12/12 OK. Validates the operator's canonical use case (parallel panel across multiple engines for cross-engine review). BUG DISCOVERED + FIXED: first run crashed on KeyError 'rate_limited' in the aggregation step because the subprocess.TimeoutExpired branch in _dispatch_one was missing the rate_limited key.  Fixed in the script + ran resume to capture the missing 5 cells. ARTIFACTS: coord/reviews/swarm-stress-2026-05-26/{SYNTHESIS.md, curve_rows.csv, mixed_results.json}. STRESS-TEST ARC: previously the audit panel said concurrency >10x would need higher-volume testing to validate.  This closes that. Next-level stress (100+ concurrency, real Token-Plan credit pool burn, multi-day soak) remains queued but is no longer load-bearing for the operator's confidence in 32-way panels.

Title: Daily-driver cross-engine panel verb: harness ask 'question'
Notes: Single-command operator-facing cross-engine panel.  Packages today's infrastructure (pool-aware dispatch + health-aware key selection + automatic engine failover) behind one CLI verb. SHIPPED: src/harness/ask.py - run_panel() + save_panel() + AskResult dataclass.  Uses dispatch_with_pool internally so every panel call exercises the multi-key resolver + health ledger + auto-failover.  Saves question.md + per-engine .md + summary.json + packet.md (synthesis-ready bundle) under coord/reviews/ask-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<slug>/. CLI: harness ask 'question'.  Options: --file (question from file), --engines (comma-list, default = 3 Pattern B engines), --output (custom dir), --max-budget-usd (per-engine cap, default $0.30), --timeout-s (default 180), --no-save, --print-text (full responses to stdout). DESIGN CHOICE: does NOT auto-synthesize.  Surfaces 3 independent perspectives + saves a packet.md ready for operator review or in-session Claude synthesis.  Auto-synthesis can be added later if workflow demands it. TESTS: 22 new in test_ask.py - TestSlugify (5), TestRunPanel (4 - all-engines, order-preservation, failure, exception), TestSavePanel (6 - question.md, per-engine, summary.json, packet.md, failed-result, creates-dir), TestAskCli (7 - help, requires-arg, from-arg, from-file, saves-to-dir, print-text, fail-exit-code). Used the lazy-import escape hatch so dispatch_with_pool isn't imported at module load (matches the existing pattern in dispatch.py and avoids circular issues).  Tests patch at the source module (harness.engines.pool_dispatch.dispatch_with_pool) rather than harness.ask. COST PROFILE: typical 3-engine audit-class panel ~$0.20-0.30 with MiMo + DeepSeek-flash + Kimi.  On the MiMo TP economics post-2026-05-26 price cut, a single panel costs less than 1/6000 of the $50/month plan. USAGE: harness ask 'should we deprecate X?' produces a coord/reviews/ask-* dir the operator (or next session) can review.

Title: Deployment evaluation + 3 quick-win docs (.env.example, OPERATOR_QUICKSTART, README rewrite)
Notes: Operator request: evaluate how well-documented we are for deploying on a new machine; consider whether xaxiu-swarm also needs to be cloned; identify friction for non-technical users; ship quick wins. AUDIT FINDINGS via Explore subagent: scored existing deploy docs (README, CLAUDE.md, MIGRATION.md, docs/, pyproject.toml, CLI verbs).  6 specific friction points identified: (1) no .env.example template, (2) xaxiu-swarm dependency invisible, (3) no operator-facing quickstart (existing AGENT_QUICKSTART is for AI agents not humans), (4) PATH update manual + requires shell restart, (5) no Linux/Mac installer scripts, (6) no unified setup wizard. QUICK WINS SHIPPED (this commit): (a) .env.example - repo-root template with all 7 provider env vars + multi-key pool convention + comments pointing at harness keys serve. Solves 'what env vars do I need?' forever. (b) docs/OPERATOR_QUICKSTART.md - 250-line non-technical operator guide.  Blank machine -> first dispatch in 6 numbered steps.  Documents: prereqs (Python, Git, claude CLI), pip install, harness keys serve for browser-based key entry, harness doctor for verification, harness ask for first cross-engine panel, optional wrapper-script install, optional xaxiu-swarm second-repo clone, daily-use commands, common-issues FAQ, filesystem map.  Sits at docs/ to be discoverable. (c) README.md Quick start section rewritten - now starts with harness keys serve (operator-friendly browser form), uses python -m harness universal form, adds harness ask example alongside harness dispatch, includes a NEW 'Optional second repo: xaxiu-swarm' section explaining WHEN you'd want it + how to install via pip or uv tool.  Closes the swarm-dependency-invisible friction. TWO-REPO STORY CLARIFIED: xaxiu-harness REQUIRED for everything operator does. xaxiu-swarm OPTIONAL - only needed for agentic swarm dispatch (swarm/* backends).  Operator's primary use case (harness ask, dispatch_with_pool, Pattern B engines) works without it. EVALUATION REPORT: coord/reviews/deployment-evaluation-2026-05-26/EVALUATION.md documents the audit + remaining friction + recommended future improvements ranked by leverage. TIME-TO-PRODUCTIVE: experienced dev with prereqs ~5-10min, non-technical operator ~25-40min, blank-machine ~60-90min. Today's quick wins improve the non-technical path by ~10-15min and clarify the swarm dependency. REMAINING FRICTION (queued, not blocking): harness setup interactive wizard (~4-6h, biggest UX gain), claude binary check in harness doctor (~15min), bin/install-harness.sh for Linux/Mac (~3-4h), harness keys set <provider> <value> (~2h unified config). No tests added (docs-only change).

Title: harness doctor now checks claude CLI binary (Pattern B prerequisite)
Notes: Operator question: when deploying harness, is it preferred to deploy inside Claude Code app, or have a CLI for harness with Claude Code ready-on by default? STRATEGIC ANSWER: harness as standalone CLI is the right model. Claude Code as one of harness's tools (Pattern B subprocess), NOT harness as a tool inside Claude Code.  This keeps harness fast, cheap, scriptable in CI/cron, and avoids double-billing. TACTICAL FIX: harness doctor previously had 6 checks but didn't verify the claude CLI is installed -- the prerequisite for ALL Pattern B engines (kimi-via-claude / mimo-via-claude / deepseek-via-claude) and ALL wrapper scripts. Actionable fix hint points at docs.claude.com setup page.  Probes --version on success path to confirm the binary actually runs (not just that it's on PATH). TESTS: 5 new in TestClaudeBinaryCheck - warn-when-missing, ok-when-version-succeeds, warn-when-version-fails, doctor-run-all-includes-claude-check, doctor-cli-surfaces-claude-check. LIVE VERIFY on this machine: harness doctor now shows 'claude installed: 2.1.150 (Claude Code)' as the 3rd check.

Title: Interactive harness setup wizard for fresh-machine onboarding
Notes: Biggest single UX gain for non-technical operators (per W14-DEPLOY-QUICKWINS evaluation).  Single command that walks operator from blank machine to first successful dispatch with explicit consent at every step. SHIPPED: src/harness/setup_wizard.py + harness setup CLI verb.  5 steps: (1) harness doctor preflight diagnostics (2) Claude Code CLI availability check with install hint (3) API key configuration (offers to launch harness keys serve) (4) Wrapper script installation (claude-mimo / claude-kimi / claude-deepseek) (5) Smoke dispatch (verifies end-to-end wiring with a trivial 'say OK' through the first configured engine). DESIGN PRINCIPLES: consent-gated (every action prompts, default is the safe choice), idempotent (safe to re-run; skips done steps), no magic (never modifies PATH, no sudo, no writes outside repo + ~/.harness). NON-INTERACTIVE MODE: --non-interactive flag accepts safe defaults at every prompt + skips browser-opening + skips real dispatch.  Suitable for CI / scripted bootstrap. TESTS: 16 new in test_setup_wizard.py - TestStepDoctor (2), TestStepClaudeBinary (2), TestStepKeys (2), TestStepWrappers (2), TestStepSmokeDispatch (3), TestRunWizard (2), TestSetupCli (3). Title: Handoff sharing-kit doc with Claude Code setup prompt for recipients
Notes: Operator request: produce the workflow for handing harness to an end user.  'Clone this repo, pass this to your Claude Code for setup, here is how to trigger it in the future.' SHIPPED: docs/HANDOFF.md - one-page sharing kit. STRUCTURE: Part 1 (sharer-facing): three things to send the recipient - (A) the repo URL, (B) a short orientation message they can paste into Slack/email/chat, (C) the EXACT Claude Code setup prompt to paste into their session. Part 2 (recipient-facing): step-by-step what they do - install prereqs (Python 3.11+ / Git / Claude Code CLI), clone, open Claude Code in the directory, paste the setup prompt, get a provider key. Part 3 (daily use): the 4 commands they need after setup + where outputs live (coord/reviews/ask-*/ map) + how to debug via Claude Code prompts. Part 4 (operator-facing FAQ): pre-emptive answers to likely follow-up questions (update, backup, uninstall, where's docs). Optional add-on: when/how to mention the xaxiu-swarm sibling. THE CLAUDE CODE SETUP PROMPT IS THE STAR: 8-step prompt that tells the recipient's Claude Code session to: read CLAUDE.md + OPERATOR_QUICKSTART, check Python/Git/Claude Code prereqs, pip install -e ., run harness doctor, launch harness keys serve --no-open, wait for keys paste, re-run doctor, do a verification dispatch via harness ask, then print the 3 daily-use commands.  CRITICAL constraint at the end: 'do NOT try to fix failures by running arbitrary commands or editing .env / config; tell me the error + likely cause, let me decide. My machine is not a sandbox.' README updated to put HANDOFF.md as the top entry-point doc pointer (above VISUAL_MANUAL, OPERATOR_QUICKSTART, RUNBOOK, AGENT_QUICKSTART).  Now 5 entry-point pointers ordered by use case. TIME ESTIMATES (for the recipient): 15-25 min if they have Python + Claude Code + a provider account already.  Up to ~60 min if they need to sign up for providers. No tests (docs only).

Title: harness agent-instructions verb + docs/USING_HARNESS_FROM_OTHER_PROJECTS.md
Notes: Operator question: how does an agent know the harness exists after the operator is using it from a DIFFERENT project? Where is the harness installed? Will the operator have access from a new project or need to point it out? ANSWERED + SHIPPED 3 things: (1) New CLI verb: harness agent-instructions --format {claude-md, prompt, short}.  Prints a snippet that teaches any new Claude Code session that the harness is available + how to use it.  Absolute install path is baked in (not a placeholder) so the agent knows exactly where the install lives.  Three formats: claude-md (full section to drop into user CLAUDE.md), prompt (one-shot for new sessions), short (one-paragraph hint). (2) New module: src/harness/cli.py:agent_instructions_cmd uses Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] to compute the repo root + Path.home() for the wrapper dir.  Single source of truth for both paths. (3) New doc: docs/USING_HARNESS_FROM_OTHER_PROJECTS.md explains: where everything installs (Python source, egg-link, CLI binary, .env, coord/, ~/.harness/), the 'harness follows you' model (works from any cwd; outputs land in harness repo not the calling project), the 3 options for teaching new agents (user-level CLAUDE.md, per-project CLAUDE.md, one-shot paste), patterns for use, limitations + edge cases (multiple venvs, multiple machines, moving the repo). TESTS: 6 new in test_agent_instructions.py - help, claude-md-format-default, short-format, prompt-format, bakes-in-absolute-install-path (no placeholders), invalid-format-rejected. USAGE PATTERN: 'harness agent-instructions --format claude-md >> ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md' - one command makes the harness available to every Claude Code session on the machine, in any project directory.  Operator never has to tell each new session that the harness exists. Doc ladder in README now has 6 entry points (HANDOFF, VISUAL_MANUAL, OPERATOR_QUICKSTART, USING_HARNESS_FROM_OTHER_PROJECTS, INTERNAL_OPERATOR_RUNBOOK, AGENT_QUICKSTART).

Title: Dashboard refuses non-loopback bind (was unauthenticated LAN-visible)
Notes: External audit P4 MEDIUM: src/harness/dashboard/app.py:126-287 exposes /api/state, /api/cost, /api/queue, /api/l5-events, /ws with NO token/auth (contrast keys_ui.py which is token-gated).  cli.py:2028 defaulted --host 127.0.0.1 but let the operator override it; dashboard/server.py:14 passed host straight to uvicorn.run.  --host 0.0.0.0 would have exposed operational data (engine activity, costs, observer flags, L5 events) to the LAN unauthenticated.  FIX: added LOOPBACK_HOSTS = {127.0.0.1, ::1, localhost} constant + NonLoopbackBindRefused exception class in dashboard/server.py.  serve() now validates host against this set BEFORE calling uvicorn.run; non-loopback values raise NonLoopbackBindRefused with a clear message naming the offending host AND explaining the auth gap.  CLI surface (dashboard-serve command) catches the exception and re-raises as ClickException so the operator gets a clean non-zero exit with the same explanation.  --host help text documents the loopback requirement.  Default 127.0.0.1 unchanged.  TESTS: 7 new in TestNonLoopbackBindRefused covering serve() accepts (127.0.0.1, localhost, ::1), serve() refuses (0.0.0.0, 192.168.1.10), CLI refuses 0.0.0.0 with non-zero exit + readable error, --help documents requirement.  Full non-slow suite green at 2870 passed (net +7).  Live smoke: python -m harness dashboard-serve --host 0.0.0.0 exits 1 with 'dashboard host 0.0.0.0 is not a loopback address; ... exposes operational state to your LAN'.

Title: Dependency upper bounds + requirements.lock (was bare >= floors)
Notes: External audit P5 MEDIUM: pyproject.toml:12-33 had every dep as a bare >= floor (no upper bounds) and no lockfile.  The documented first action every session is fresh pip install -e ., so a breaking-or-malicious transitive release would be pulled automatically onto a machine holding paid-API keys.  FIX: (1) Added major-version upper bounds to all 10 runtime deps + 4 dev deps in pyproject.toml — generous bounds that refuse a known-incompatible MAJOR bump (click<10, pydantic<3, pyyaml<7, fastapi<1, uvicorn<1, httpx<1, websockets<20, rich<20, psutil<10, pypdf<10; pytest<10, pytest-asyncio<2, ruff<1, mypy<2).  Bounds are conservative (one major) not aggressive (one minor) since the operator runs pip install -e . on session resume + doesn't want spurious 'dep not resolvable' on a routine upgrade.  (2) Committed requirements.lock — focused 28-package transitive closure built in a fresh .audit-tmp-venv (python -m pip install -e .), then frozen with --exclude xaxiu-harness so the editable install of self doesn't leak.  Header documents how to use the lockfile (pip install -r requirements.lock + pip install -e . --no-deps) and how to regenerate (.tmp-lock venv recipe).  (3) Added .gitignore entries for .tmp-lock/ + .audit-tmp-venv/ so future regen runs don't accidentally commit the scratch venv.  TESTS: 8/8 install-smoke + install-verify tests green; full non-slow suite 2870 passed unchanged from P4.

Title: SHA-256 + HMAC tamper-evident chain for audit JSONL ledger (security panel #1 pick)
Notes: Closes the audit-integrity gap. Strategic panel's #1 security recommendation (0.90/0.95 confidence). Without this, any process with write access to ~/.harness/audit.jsonl can rewrite history post-hoc — flip success: true to false, change engine names, edit redacted excerpts — with no signal. AUDIT-FIRST per feedback_grep_before_declare_greenfield_2026_05_28: grep'd hmac|HMAC|audit_chain across src/harness/ — only hits were unrelated TOS-compliance language about 'client-identity tampering' in engines/concrete.py + claude_code_subprocess.py + provenance.py spec-tampering detection (different feature — spec sha for adapter configs, not audit ledger). audit_jsonl.py had ZERO chain code. Truly greenfield. NEW: src/harness/audit_chain.py (~290 LOC). Each audit event now carries prev_hash (prior entry's hmac, or GENESIS sentinel) + hmac (HMAC-SHA256 keyed off a stored secret, computed over canonical JSON of event excluding the hmac field itself). canonical_json() uses sort_keys + tight separators + ensure_ascii=False for byte-stable reproduction. compute_hmac() excludes the hmac field from its own input (non-circular). chain_event() returns a new dict with prev_hash + hmac added (no mutation). get_last_chain_hash() reads file backwards to find the most recent hmac. get_hmac_key() resolves via env var HARNESS_AUDIT_HMAC_KEY first, then DPAPI secret of the same name (Windows only) with auto-generate-and-persist on first use; non-Windows hosts without env var return None and the chain silently degrades (events write without chain fields). KEY DESIGN: best-effort policy mirrors existing redact_secrets — chain failures NEVER block dispatch. verify_chain() returns ChainVerifyResult dataclass with ok / total / chained / legacy / chain_restarts (tuple of line numbers where chain restarts; expected at file start + any post-prune resumption) / first_tamper_line / reason / key_available. WIRED: audit_jsonl.py::append_dispatch_event now calls chain_event before writing each entry; failures caught + swallowed (event still writes uncheained). CLI: harness audit verify subcommand (new) under existing audit group — emits PASS/FAIL with line number of first tamper + reason. JSON output via --format json. Exit code 0 = OK, 1 = tampered. TESTS: 34 new in test_audit_chain.py (TestCanonicalJSON × 4 sorted-keys/separators/unicode/nested + TestComputeHmac × 4 hmac-excludes-itself/reproducible/key-changes-output/field-changes-output + TestChainEvent × 3 adds-fields/no-mutation/links + TestGetHmacKey × 4 hex/raw/short-hex/no-env-no-dpapi + TestGetLastChainHash × 4 missing/empty/legacy-only/returns-most-recent + TestVerifyChain × 11 missing-file/empty/legacy-only/clean-chain/tampered-hmac/tampered-payload/tampered-prev-hash/legacy-then-chained-restart/malformed-lines/no-key-advisory/post-prune-restart + TestIntegrationWithAppendEvent × 4 chain-fields-written/two-appends-chain/manual-tamper-detected/no-key-legacy-format). Live smoke test: 3 chained events with kimi/deepseek/mimo show GENESIS→hmac1→hmac2→hmac3 linkage + verify_chain returns ok=True chained=3. Existing 2289 entries in ~/.harness/audit.jsonl correctly identified as legacy (no chain fields) — no false-positives. THREAT MODEL covered: detect post-hoc tampering by a process with write access. NOT in scope: replay attacks, dropped-entry detection (prune compatibility documented as acceptable cost), forged entries (BLOCKED — attacker needs the HMAC key which sits in DPAPI).
